zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Young於1475年出生在英國越可爾時，她的父親William Young20歲，母親Janet Elspeth Hastings17歲。她於1495年與John James Ellithorpe Watson在英國英格蘭越可爾克利夫蘭結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1528年在她的家鄉享年53歲，埋葬在美國賓夕法尼亞州薩默塞特。

nameMeanings.name: Young
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and northern Irish: nickname from Middle English yong ‘young’ (Old English geong), used to distinguish a younger man from an older man bearing the same personal name (typically, father and son). In Middle English this name is often found with the Anglo-Norman French definite article, for example Robert le Yunge. In Gaelic-speaking areas of Scotland this was widely used as an English equivalent of the Gaelic nickname Og ‘young’; see Ogg . This surname is also very common among African Americans.

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘young’ or similar, notably German   Jung  , Dutch   Jong   and   De Jong  , and French   Lejeune   and   Lajeunesse  .
Americanized form of Swedish Ljung: topographic or an ornamental name from ljung ‘(field of) heather’, or a habitational name from a placename containing this word, e.g. Ljungby.

variants.name: Yongue
meanings: English: variant of   Young  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.
Perhaps also an Americanized form of Dutch   De Jonge  .

variants.name: Youngs
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of Young , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Younger
meanings: English (mainly Northumberland and eastern Scotland): from Middle English yungre ‘younger’, comparative of Young , used to distinguish a younger man from an older man bearing the same personal name (typically, father and son). In one case, at least, however, the name is known to have been borne by an immigrant Fleming, and was probably an Americanized form of Middle Dutch jongheer ‘young nobleman’ (see Jonker ).
Americanized form of various cognate or like-sounding names in other languages, notably German   Junger   and   Junker  , or Dutch   Jonker  .

variants.name: Ogg
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of a nickname from the Gaelic adjective óg ‘young’, used to distinguish the junior of two bearers of the same personal name (typically, father and son).
English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Hogg  , pronounced without initial /h/.

variants.name: Younge
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Fitzroy, Sinead.

English: variant of   Young  . — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 823, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Young was born in 1475, in Yorkshire, England, her father, William Young, was 20 and her mother, Janet Elspeth Hastings, was 17. She married John James Ellithorpe Watson in 1495, in Cleveland, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1528, in her hometown, at the age of 53, and was buried in Somerset, Pennsylvania, United States.

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Young(이)가 1475년 영국 요르크실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Young(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Janet Elspeth Hastings(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 1495년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 클레벨랜드에서 John James Ellithorpe Watson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1528년 그녀의 고향에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 펜실베이니아 주 서머세트에 묻혔다.

ru.Holme-on-Spalding-Moor, East Riding of Y: холме-он-спалдинг-мур, еркшир эаст ридинг, Англия, Великобритания
холме-он-спалдинг-мур, еркшир эаст ридинг, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Young родилась в 1475 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени William Young было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Janet Elspeth Hastings, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John James Ellithorpe Watson в 1495 г. в местности кливеланд, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1528 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 53 лет и была похоронена в местности сомирсет, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты.

es.Spalding Moor, Yorkshire, England: Low Moor, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes Young nació en 1475, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William Young, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Janet Elspeth Hastings, tenía 17 años. Se casó con John James Ellithorpe Watson en 1495, en Cleveland, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1528, en su pueblo, a la edad de 53 años, y fue sepultada en Somerset, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos.
